reason: Cannot sustain a refutation: the change is PASS today (baseline recall 1.00 / fp 0.00 across 3 repeats on both bad fixtures + the good fixture), block_threshold stays 'high', and the two demotions (nullability, mutation-design info->low) only touch ADVISORY categories that no bad fixture relies on. Verified the bad fixtures are detected via the SEPARATE breaking-change clause, not the demoted clauses: graphql-rest-divergence (name->title rename) and graphql-mutation-input-mismatch (adding required non-null `description: str` arg) both flag 'high' as 'breaking schema change WITHOUT a compatible path' in all 3 baseline runs (findings/api-design/bad/*__r{0,1,2}.json line 18/43). So re-mapping mutation/nullability to low does NOT let either known-bad fixture slip. The fixture edits are responsible, not refutable: the change itself flags good/graphql-rest-aligned as WEAK/DEGENERATE (it is a REST-only diff touching routes/items.py, not the GraphQL schema -- confirmed in change.patch -- so it tests scope-obedience, not GraphQL judgment) and ADDS a genuine GraphQL additive good fixture rather than tuning against the dirty one; and renames bad/graphql-rest-divergence (the patch is a pure GraphQL field rename, no REST in it) to match its real content. ONE concrete hazard worth flagging to the controller (a wording nit, not a ship-blocker): the rewrite phrase 're-map the two flag-as-info categories (nullability, mutation-design) to LOW ... never block on them' must NOT be read as demoting the breaking-change sub-case 'tightening a nullable field to non-null' -- that is the exact mechanism by which the graphql-mutation-input-mismatch non-null tightening is caught at high. The original prompt keeps breaking-changes (rename/remove/tighten-to-non-null) as a distinct high clause; the controller must preserve that clause verbatim and only demote the residual style-level nullability/mutation-design observations. With the breaking-change clause intact the under-flag risk is eliminated, so the change ships.
